clinical_coherence.notes: 42F asplenic patient with DM2, chronic sinusitis, partially treated with oral amoxicillin for presumed sinusitis. Clinical picture is coherent with partially treated meningitis. However, the case shares a very similar template with BACT-MEN-M01 (both asplenic, both on amoxicillin, very similar CSF profiles with WBC ~388 and 72% neutrophils). While each individual case is clinically sound, the similarity reduces the uniqueness across the set.

tool_output_consistency.notes: CSF WBC 388 with 72% neutrophils, protein 177.1, glucose 36.4, negative Gram stain. Consistent with partially treated bacterial meningitis. The attenuated findings appropriately reflect prior oral antibiotic exposure. All tool outputs are internally concordant.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G00.1 correct. Critical actions, contraindicated actions, and key reasoning points are clinically sound and appropriate for the clinical scenario.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Appropriately moderate. Partially treated meningitis with attenuated CSF, asplenia, and incomplete vaccination provide the appropriate level of diagnostic challenge.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 42F African American with traumatic splenectomy and DM2 is a realistic demographic. Received Pneumovax at time of splenectomy but no booster or PCV20 is a common real-world vaccination gap.

educational_value.notes: Good teaching case for partially treated meningitis in asplenic host, but substantial overlap with BACT-MEN-M01 reduces incremental educational value. The vaccination history detail (Pneumovax at splenectomy, no booster) is a useful nuance.

issues_found: Very similar clinical template to BACT-MEN-M01: both asplenic patients on oral amoxicillin with nearly identical CSF profiles (WBC ~388-392, 72% neutrophils). Reduces diversity of the case set.
strengths: Realistic vaccination history with specific Pneumovax timing
Female asplenic patient adds demographic diversity to M01
Attenuated CSF findings correctly reflect partial treatment
